candidates.headline: Led 125-person aerospace operations with $144M output across CNC machining, assembly, and military platforms.
candidates.career_trajectory: Aerospace operations — CNC machining, military platforms

edc_data.motivation_hook: Seeks stable aerospace culture after two PE-driven eliminations; prioritizes fit over maximum pay.

scope_match.candidate_actual: 125 hourly employees + 5 supervisors at Collins Aerospace; 116 hourly + 8 supervisors at GE Aviation
scope_match.role_requirement: Engineering team, programming team direct; production supervisor and supply chain dotted line across 75-person site

scope_match.candidate_actual: $144M yearly output goal at Collins Aerospace; $53M at GE Aviation with $3.3M tooling budget ownership
scope_match.role_requirement: $30M site revenue with VP/GM retaining P&L ownership; Director provides operational execution

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reported to VP overseeing 3-4 divisions at Collins; reported to Site Leader at GE Aviation and Woodward
scope_match.role_requirement: Reports to VP/General Manager who oversees entire St. Louis site

scope_match.candidate_actual: Single-site operations at Rockford facilities; managed two-building footprint at GE Aviation
scope_match.role_requirement: St. Louis site with two buildings 3 blocks apart; serves as liaison to Florida site

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Jose brings larger-scale operations experience from Collins Aerospace ($144M output, 125 employees) and GE Aviation ($53M output, 116 employees) to Kemco’s $30M, 75-person site. His progression through business unit leadership at multi-division organizations positions him to provide structure and guidance while working under a VP/GM, though he has historically owned full P&L accountability rather than dotted-line production oversight. His two-building management experience at GE Aviation and cross-site coordination at Collins directly translate to Kemco’s split-building footprint and Florida liaison requirements.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace experience
key_criteria.evidence: Jose has spent 100% of his 20+ year career in aerospace and defense manufacturing, progressing from CNC machinist apprentice through director roles at GE Aviation, Woodward, Collins Aerospace, and Kadon Aerospace manufacturing components for military and commercial aircraft including de-icing valves, generators, and defense platforms.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at GE Aviation, Collins Aerospace, Woodward

key_criteria.name: Operations/production experience with engineering/technical exposure/credibility
key_criteria.evidence: Jose spent 10-12 years as hands-on CNC machinist running mills, lathes, grinders, and manual machines before transitioning to operations leadership. At GE Aviation, the site leader created the Director of Operations role specifically for him because he had worked in manufacturing engineering, quality management, and every office in the facility. Jose led business unit operations at Collins Aerospace managing 125 hourly employees, 5 supervisors, and $144M yearly output with dotted-line responsibility for manufacturing engineering, quality, and continuous improvement.

key_criteria.name: CNC machining experience
key_criteria.evidence: Jose significantly exceeds the 50% threshold with approximately 60% of his career in CNC machining, including 12 years as hands-on machinist and lead operator running CNC mills, lathes, grinders, and lapping machines, followed by director roles overseeing machine shop operations in low-volume high-mix environments.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Woodward, Hamilton Sundstrand, GE Aviation

key_criteria.name: Continuous improvement experience
key_criteria.evidence: Jose is a certified Black Belt who delivered $1.25M in recognized savings as dedicated Lean Leader at GE Aviation, achieving 75% lead time reduction and 66% delinquency reduction. He has consistently embedded CI into operations roles, delivering $10M delinquency reduction at Woodward and $2M at Collins Aerospace.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at GE Aviation, Woodward, Collins Aerospace

key_criteria.name: Personality and culture fit — outgoing and personable
key_criteria.evidence: Jose reduced union grievances by 85% in two years at Collins Aerospace by proactively opening communication channels, giving union representatives his cell phone number, and addressing concerns on the floor. He describes himself as a boots-on-ground leader who goes in early and stays late to engage operators on every shift.

compensation.current_total: Currently unemployed. Most recent role at Kadon Aerospace: Not disclosed. Prior role at Collins Aerospace: $200,000+ base with bonus (total not specified)
compensation.flexibility: Significant flexibility demonstrated. Jose is willing to accept $170,000 base versus previous $200,000+ at Collins Aerospace, stating he prioritizes job satisfaction over maximum compensation now that his children are becoming independent and he no longer has college cost pressures. He confirmed $170k base is acceptable and expressed openness to negotiating bonus percentage.

edc_data.notice_period: Immediately available - currently unemployed since November 2025

why_interested.headline: Passion for aerospace and boots-on-ground operations leadership
why_interested.detail: Jose explicitly stated aviation is his passion and expressed strong alignment with Kemco’s low-volume high-mix CNC machining environment serving 90% military platforms for Boeing and Lockheed Martin. He values the boots-on-ground director role where his technical credibility and floor presence will matter daily, noting his preference for being on the production floor engaging with operators across all shifts.

why_interested.headline: Cultural fit with tight-knit, stable organization
why_interested.detail: Jose expressed strong attraction to Kemco’s tight-knit community and low turnover, stating that 100-150 employee organizations are his comfort zone. He values collaborative environments and emphasized seeking a place he enjoys working rather than chasing maximum compensation, particularly now that his children are becoming independent and he no longer has college cost pressures.

why_interested.headline: Two consecutive position eliminations at PE-backed organizations
why_interested.detail: Jose’s position at Kadon Aerospace was eliminated after six months when a retiring director decided to remain. Prior to that, his role at Collins Aerospace ended after two years due to organizational restructuring. Both eliminations were due to external factors rather than performance, but the pattern has motivated him to seek a more stable environment.

why_interested.headline: Opportunity to build continuous improvement capability from ground up
why_interested.detail: Jose sees alignment with Kemco’s early-stage lean journey and consultant-supported approach, having previously been hired as the first dedicated Lean Leader at GE Aviation to build CI capability from scratch. He views this as an opportunity to apply his Black Belt expertise and proven track record delivering $1.25M savings and 75% lead time reduction to establish structured continuous improvement processes.

potential_concerns: Two consecutive position eliminations within one year (Kadon Aerospace after 6 months in November 2025, Collins Aerospace after 2 years) may raise client questions about tenure stability, though both eliminations were due to external organizational factors rather than performance issues.

Tendency toward overly detailed, rambling communication style during interview may require coaching for executive-level conciseness in client-facing situations, though this appears to stem from genuine enthusiasm and thoroughness rather than poor communication skills.

Rockford, Illinois to St. Louis commute (approximately 3.5-4 hours) would initially be solo arrangement without family until son finishes high school in two months. While Jose expressed willingness to commute and familiarity with St. Louis, the sustainability of this arrangement long-term should be validated, particularly given his emphasis on work-life balance and family considerations.

our_take.text: We believe Jose is a genuinely enthusiastic and technically credible operations leader who would bring valuable aerospace machining expertise and continuous improvement capability to Kemco, though his tendency to provide lengthy, detailed explanations may require managing in client-facing situations. His passion for aviation, boots-on-ground leadership style, and proven ability to build collaborative relationships in union environments align well with Kemco’s culture, and his compensation flexibility and willingness to commute demonstrate strong motivation for the right opportunity.

candidates.headline: Ranken-trained machinist running a 100-person, $27M precision CNC site after six years on the same shop floor.
candidates.career_trajectory: Precision CNC operations — automotive and prior aerospace shop-floor exposure

edc_data.motivation_hook: Open to a lateral move; prioritises fit and team quality over compensation.

scope_match.candidate_actual: 100 people at Deutsche Precision with five direct reports (controller, HR manager, plant manager, operations manager, production manager); previously six years as Plant Manager at the same site
scope_match.role_requirement: Engineering team, programming team direct; production supervisor and supply chain dotted line across 75-person site

scope_match.candidate_actual: Full P&L ownership as GM at Deutsche Precision ($27M revenue) and at Liberty Tire Recycling for nearly six years
scope_match.role_requirement: $30M site revenue with VP/GM retaining P&L ownership; Director provides operational execution

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reports to COO of a five-location, Ohio-headquartered organisation at Deutsche Precision
scope_match.role_requirement: Reports to VP/General Manager who oversees entire St. Louis site

scope_match.candidate_actual: Single-site operations at Deutsche Precision in Greater St. Louis; Rob noted Kemco's Kirkwood site is 'not far from my house at all'
scope_match.role_requirement: St. Louis site with two buildings 3 blocks apart; serves as liaison to Florida site

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Rob brings full GM-level scope from a $27M, 100-person precision CNC site to Kemco's $30M, 75-person operation — comparable headcount, comparable revenue. He has held full P&L accountability for six consecutive years (Liberty Tire then Deutsche Precision), which is broader than the Kemco role's operational-execution remit under a VP/GM. His current reporting line is to a remote multi-site COO rather than an on-site VP/GM. Local geography is a clear positive: he lives close to the Kirkwood site and previously worked there.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace experience
key_criteria.evidence: Rob's only direct aerospace exposure was 13 months as Production Supervisor at Kemco (May 2009–June 2010) — roughly 6% of his 17-year career. The balance has been precision CNC for automotive (Deutsche Precision) and manufacturing for big-box retail (Liberty Tire Recycling). No stated experience with OEMs or other tier-1/tier-2 aerospace suppliers.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Kemco, Deutsche Precision, Liberty Tire Recycling

key_criteria.name: Operations/production experience with engineering/technical exposure/credibility
key_criteria.evidence: Rob holds a Journeyman Machinist certification from Ranken Technical College, the credential Tara has previously called 'the most credible machining qualification in the St. Louis market' (also held by Imboden and Kottemann). His career progressed machining → engineering → project management, then 6 years 9 months as Plant Manager at Deutsche Precision before returning as GM in February 2026.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Ranken Technical College, Deutsche Precision

key_criteria.name: CNC machining experience
key_criteria.evidence: Approximately 11–12 years of Rob's 17-year career has been in CNC machining: Ranken certification (1998–2000), Project Engineer at Hydromat (4+ years across two tenures), Production Supervisor at Kemco, and 6 years 9 months as Plant Manager at Deutsche Precision. Current operation runs Hydromat rotary transfer machines, which Rob noted are 'different' from milling centres or conventional lathes.

key_criteria.name: Continuous improvement experience
key_criteria.evidence: Rob describes CI as 'a big part of my career' and is currently automating data-entry tasks at Deutsche Precision using AI tools, but holds no formal lean or Six Sigma certification. Approach is practical and problem-solving rather than methodology-driven — a gap against Kemco's early-lean-journey, value-add profile.

key_criteria.name: Personality and culture fit — outgoing and personable
key_criteria.evidence: Rob is currently customer-facing at Deutsche Precision and was 'very involved with the customer' during his prior tenure there. Communication during interview was clear, professional, and people-oriented, with openness about compensation, notice complexity, and motivation. Interview format limits full assessment of outgoing energy.

compensation.current_bonus: 15% annual bonus target (~$31,875 if fully achieved); Rob described the goal as 'very difficult to get to' and 'very aggressive' this year as he was not part of the budgeting process
compensation.current_total: $212,500 base, total cash range approximately $212,500–$244,375 with bonus; no equity or long-term incentive
compensation.expected_total: Open to lateral move at $180,000 base for right opportunity
compensation.flexibility: Notable flexibility. Rob stated 'I'm at a point in my career...I'm not at a point where I'm taking jobs just for money anymore' and confirmed he would consider $180k base if 'the job itself and the team I'm working with all make sense.' He asked about Kemco's salary band early in the conversation and did not push back when given the $180k starting point.

edc_data.notice_period: Approximately 2 months (driven by customer-facing handoff at Deutsche Precision); Rob said he would 'try to work with them a little bit' for a shorter timeline depending on Kemco's urgency

why_interested.headline: Return to CNC machining and aerospace after detours into automotive and tire
why_interested.detail: Rob is a Ranken-trained machinist whose technical depth lies in precision CNC and aerospace. After nearly six years at Liberty Tire (recycled tire products for Lowe's, Home Depot, Menards) and three months back at Deutsche Precision (single-customer automotive), Kemco represents a return to the kind of work his certification and early career align with.

why_interested.headline: Local familiarity with the Kemco site
why_interested.detail: Rob worked Kemco's shop floor for 13 months as Production Supervisor (2009–2010) and described his departure as being 'for growth' rather than conflict. He noted the Kirkwood site is 'not far from my house at all.' He appeared unaware of the Crestview ownership change, suggesting no maintained ties — but his familiarity with the site and potentially long-tenure employees could accelerate onboarding.

why_interested.headline: Single-customer commercial exposure at Deutsche Precision
why_interested.detail: Deutsche Precision's $27M operation serves a single automotive customer. Narrow commercial exposure relative to the multi-customer aerospace environment Kemco operates in.

potential_concerns: Limited aerospace experience — only 13 months at Kemco 16 years ago, roughly 6% of career. The balance is automotive precision CNC and big-box retail manufacturing. This is a notable gap against the 'aerospace-dominant throughout' profile.

Very recent start at Deutsche Precision (3 months, since February 2026). Leaving after 3 months creates tenure optics with future references and complicates notice negotiation given his customer-facing handoff requirements.

Prior Kemco tenure (2009–2010) requires a quiet check with the client before progressing, including whether VP/GM Delana (10+ years tenure) remembers him and how his 2010 departure was perceived at the time.

No formal lean or Six Sigma certification. CI approach is practical and informal rather than methodology-driven, which is a gap against the 'significant value-add' CI profile Kemco is seeking as it builds out continuous improvement capability.

our_take.text: We believe Rob is a credible operations leader with the right technical foundation and local machining credibility, though his limited aerospace exposure and very recent start at Deutsche Precision require careful handling with the client, particularly given his prior Kemco tenure.

candidates.headline: 30-year aerospace engineering and CI leader at $50M MAX site; built CI from scratch and removed 3,800 constrained hours.
candidates.career_trajectory: Aerospace manufacturing engineering and operations leadership

edc_data.motivation_hook: Exploring progression to operations number-two role with customer-facing scope beyond his current engineering remit.

scope_match.candidate_actual: Approximately 55 direct and indirect reports at MAX Aerostructures across engineering, NC programming, planning, supply chain, and assembly; previously 190+ at Danfoss as Operations Site Leader
scope_match.role_requirement: Engineering team, programming team direct; production supervisor and supply chain dotted line across 75-person site

scope_match.candidate_actual: No direct P&L ownership in current role — separate director leads day-to-day operations. Held operations leadership scope at Danfoss across machining, processing, assembly, and maintenance
scope_match.role_requirement: $30M site revenue with VP/GM retaining P&L ownership; Director provides operational execution

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reports to COO at MAX Aerostructures (3 years 8 months tenure)
scope_match.role_requirement: Reports to VP/General Manager who oversees entire St. Louis site

scope_match.candidate_actual: Single-site operation in Wichita, Kansas; would require relocation to St. Louis. Discussed with wife — proximity to grandchildren is a consideration, though he noted 'sometimes they move away too'
scope_match.role_requirement: St. Louis site with two buildings 3 blocks apart; serves as liaison to Florida site

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Michael's headcount scope at MAX (~55) and previously at Danfoss (190+) brackets the Kemco role comfortably. The structural gap is functional rather than scale-related: his current MAX seat is engineering-led with a separate director owning machining and maintenance day-to-day, whereas Kemco wants the Director of Operations to own that floor accountability directly. His Danfoss tenure (May 2021 – September 2022) is the closest precedent for that kind of integrated operations role.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace experience
key_criteria.evidence: Michael presents 30 years of aerospace experience across Cessna (13 years 9 months), Bell Helicopter, Boeing (5 years 1 month as contractor), Triumph Aerostructures, OMADA International (military machined components), Danfoss, and MAX Aerostructures. Coverage spans manufacturing engineering, tooling, subassembly, final assembly, systems installation, functional testing, and flight test.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at Cessna, Bell, Boeing, Triumph, MAX Aerostructures

key_criteria.name: Operations/production experience with engineering/technical exposure/credibility
key_criteria.evidence: At Danfoss Power Solutions (2021–2022), Michael was Operations Site Leader for 190+ personnel across machining, processing, assembly, maintenance, and CI. His current MAX role is engineering-led with a separate director owning day-to-day machining and maintenance — a moderate functional gap against the Kemco operations-leader requirement, though his technical credibility on the floor is well established.

key_criteria.name: CNC machining experience
key_criteria.evidence: Michael oversees NC programming directly at MAX's low-volume high-mix CNC environment with high-speed machining and hard-metal assembly. At OMADA International he oversaw machine operations for a military machined components facility; at Danfoss he led operations for a high-volume machining, assembly, and processing site. Comfortable with both low-vol high-mix and high-volume contexts.

key_criteria.name: Continuous improvement experience
key_criteria.evidence: Michael established the CI programme from scratch at MAX Aerostructures on joining in 2022; holds two Green Belts. In the past year he removed 3,800 constrained hours from the MAG3 machine, avoiding capital expenditure on additional capacity. Prior Six Sigma project leadership and Kaizen events at Bell Helicopter and Cessna.
key_criteria.context_anchor: at MAX Aerostructures, Bell Helicopter, Cessna

key_criteria.name: Personality and culture fit — outgoing and personable
key_criteria.evidence: Michael's interview delivery was measured, thoughtful, and softly spoken rather than energetic or outgoing. He engaged substantively on role structure and asked insightful questions about COO progression and the separation of P&L from day-to-day operations. Collaborative and technically credible, but did not project the dynamic customer-facing presence Kemco has emphasised.

compensation.current_bonus: Approximately $6,000 quarterly bonus (~$1,500/quarter) plus up to $4,800 monthly morale bonus ($100–$400/month based on attendance, quality/scrap, productivity, and consumable spend, paid in cash)
compensation.expected_base: Not specifically stated — Michael indicated he would 'like to see what they kind of had in mind'
compensation.expected_bonus: Would want a meaningful bonus structure equivalent to his current quarterly bonus
compensation.expected_total: $160,000–$170,000 base range confirmed workable; likely needs the high end plus meaningful bonus to justify the move from his current ~$170k total cash
compensation.flexibility: Michael acknowledged smaller companies often cannot match OEM salaries and is open to discussion. He confirmed the $160–170k base range is 'right at the bottom number' and 'within a few thousand of where I'm at right now,' suggesting he would likely need to be at the higher end. He is not concerned about replicating the monthly morale bonus but does want quarterly bonus equivalent.

edc_data.notice_period: Minimum two weeks (his historical standard); first-quarter bonus payout (~$1,500) ideally received before starting; relocation logistics and discussion with wife may extend timeline

why_interested.headline: Progression to a customer-facing operations number-two role
why_interested.detail: Michael asked directly about whether the role could evolve toward a COO position and noted the value of separating P&L decision-making (held by VP/GM) from day-to-day operations (this role). He sees Kemco as an opportunity to step from engineering-led leadership into integrated operations accountability.

why_interested.headline: Leverage CI expertise at a site building out continuous improvement capability
why_interested.detail: Michael holds two Green Belts and built MAX's CI programme from scratch. Kemco is early in its lean journey with consultant support and no dedicated CI department, which directly maps to where he has previously delivered measurable impact (3,800 hours removed at MAG3, multiple Six Sigma projects at Bell).

why_interested.headline: Engineering-led seat at MAX with separate operations director
why_interested.detail: Michael is currently Director of Engineering & Assembly with a separate director owning machining and maintenance day-to-day. He is not in a line-operations role and is exploring positions where he would have direct operations accountability.

potential_concerns: Presentation style is measured and softly spoken rather than outgoing. Does not project the dynamic, customer-facing personality Kemco has emphasised as critical for the 'face of St. Louis' role and Florida-site liaison.

Current role at MAX is engineering-led, not operations-led — a separate director owns day-to-day machining and maintenance. Moderate functional gap against the Kemco operations-leader requirement; his Danfoss tenure (16 months) is the closest direct precedent.

Relocation from Wichita to St. Louis requires spouse discussion given proximity to grandchildren. Michael acknowledged some flexibility but timeline and personal commitment need validation before progressing.
Compensation fit may require the high end of $160–170k band plus a meaningful bonus structure to justify a move from his current ~$170k total cash.

our_take.text: We believe Michael presents strong technical credibility and continuous improvement capability, but his current engineering-focused role and measured, softly spoken presentation style may not fully align with Kemco's need for an outgoing, operations-led leader to serve as the customer-facing 'face of St. Louis.' Worth exploring further, particularly given his Danfoss operations leadership experience and his ability to establish CI infrastructure from scratch, but personality fit and compensation expectations warrant careful handling.

candidates.headline: Oversees 565-person aerospace machining operation with $91M budget across 264 production assets for Boeing's second-largest entity.
candidates.career_trajectory: Aerospace manufacturing — large-scale CNC and machining operations leadership

edc_data.headline: Oversees 565-person aerospace machining operation with $91M budget across 264 production assets for Boeing's second-largest entity.
edc_data.motivation_hook: Seeks smaller entrepreneurial environment with better work-life balance after brutal Boeing schedule.

scope_match.candidate_actual: Single site - Wichita facility (almost 1 million sq ft production space)
Reports four levels down from Site Leader in 15,000-person organization

edc_data.scope_seasoning: Justin currently manages significantly larger scope than Kemco's operation - his 565-person team with $91M budget and 264 production assets represents enterprise-scale leadership. He explicitly seeks a smaller environment and views Kemco's scale as 'more appealing,' stating downsizing isn't a concern. His progression from First Level Manager to Executive Director demonstrates adaptability across organizational scales.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace experience
key_criteria.evidence: Justin has spent 100% of his post-military career in aerospace manufacturing since 2016. Currently manages 15,000 part numbers and 175,000 monthly demand serving military and commercial platforms across tier 1 supplier and OEM operations.

key_criteria.name: Operations/production experience with engineering/technical exposure/credibility
key_criteria.evidence: Justin progressed from Production Shift Supervisor through pure operations roles to Director level. Despite no engineering degree, he developed technical depth managing 3-4-5 axis machining, NADCAP areas, and challenging programmers after self-teaching from 'couldn’t tell you a machine from a drill.'

key_criteria.name: CNC machining experience
key_criteria.evidence: Justin has managed CNC and machining for his entire eight-year aerospace career. Currently oversees 264 major production assets including 3-4-5 axis machines across hard and soft metal machining with 10,000 part numbers in low-volume high-mix environment.

key_criteria.name: Continuous improvement experience
key_criteria.evidence: Justin's CI experience is strategic rather than hands-on — he oversees continuous improvement as part of his Executive Director scope at Boeing but hasn't 'physically touched and self managed a lean type physical project' in four years. He views Kemco's early-stage CI journey as an opportunity to learn alongside the team rather than independently drive transformation.

key_criteria.name: Personality and culture fit — outgoing and personable
key_criteria.evidence: Consultant described Justin as 'real pleasant,' 'HILARIOUS,' and 'self aware' with strong interpersonal warmth. He demonstrates open communication style and relationship-building capability, maintaining close personal friendship with current boss and progressing through union environments requiring stakeholder management.

compensation.current_lti: $125,000 unvested stock over three years (approximately $45,000 per year vesting)
compensation.flexibility: Justin needs base salary 'up to $200K' as minimum threshold given $125K unvested stock he'd forfeit and dual household expenses during 3-6 month solo relocation. He's 'fine to negotiate a compelling bonus' in 20-25% range and open to performance-driven structures rather than rigid on any single component. Described himself as 'a realist' who understands most companies won't match Boeing's double-tiered bonus structure.

edc_data.notice_period: Two weeks preferred (no contractual obligation). Executive agreement did not include transition clause when Spirit became Boeing. Successor already identified who 'could take my chair tomorrow.'
edc_data.earliest_start_date: Mid-to-late May 2026. Justin wants to wait until mid-May for $20,000 stock vesting before resigning. With two weeks notice, earliest realistic start is late May or early June 2026. Would relocate solo for 3-6 months while family finishes son's baseball season.

why_interested.headline: Unsustainable work schedule and high-pressure environment
why_interested.detail: Justin works brutal 4:30 AM-4:30 PM schedule plus Saturdays, managing 1,500 overtime hours. He's 'tired of getting kicked in the teeth every day' in large corporate Boeing environment and seeks better work-life balance.

why_interested.headline: Desire for smaller entrepreneurial environment with greater impact
why_interested.detail: Justin explicitly seeks 'a smaller environment' and finds Kemco's scale 'more appealing' than his current $15B corporate entity. He wants 'a little more freedom and opportunity' to make direct impact in close-knit culture.

why_interested.headline: Family ties to St. Louis provide built-in community
why_interested.detail: Justin's wife has family connections to St. Louis, creating natural support network and community ties that ease relocation concerns beyond just the professional opportunity.

potential_concerns.concern: Significant scope reduction from 565-person, $91M enterprise operation to smaller Kemco scale may prove insufficiently challenging long-term, despite Justin's stated desire for downsizing.
$125K unvested stock walkaway creates substantial financial gap that could complicate offer negotiations or create retention risk if Justin later regrets leaving significant compensation on table.

Continuous improvement experience is more strategic than tactical - Justin hasn't 'physically touched and self managed a lean type physical project' in four years and would be learning alongside Kemco rather than independently driving CI transformation.

Competing second-round interview with Daher in Florida where wife is 'excited for the beach' creates family preference tension and timeline pressure, though Florida cost of living is twice current location.

our_take.text: We believe Justin is an exceptionally strong cultural and technical fit who brings the right blend of hands-on operations credibility, leadership scale, and interpersonal warmth. His self-awareness, humor, and genuine desire for a smaller environment with better work-life balance suggest he'd thrive in Kemco's close-knit culture and bring infectious energy to the team.
